extraction.one_line_summary: PINOT attacked on the final climb to Asiago taking stage and 10-second bonus while DUMOULIN lost 15 seconds critical time to all GC rivals.
tactical_patterns: ZAKARIN's early detonation forced MOVISTAR to chase prematurely, softening them before decisive moves on final climb.
POELS rode for stage rather than team tactics, revealing Sky's dual objectives despite GC pressure on race leader.
NIBALI repeatedly counterattacked each rival's move rather than setting sustained pace, testing everyone's reserves simultaneously.
QUINTANA required VALVERDE to pull him back after NIBALI's acceleration, exposing unexpected weakness in third-week climbing form.

rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: lost 15 seconds to all top-4 rivals on lumpy stage, suggesting pre-ITT fatigue negates time trial advantage
rider_signals.ZAKARIN Ilnur: positive: initial attack fractured peloton before final climb, showing superior tactical positioning despite missing stage win
rider_signals.POELS Wout: neutral: rode for stage instead of supporting team despite GC pressure, indicating Sky's confidence in Dumoulin's ITT margin
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: positive: won stage and 10-second bonus despite being weakest time trialist in top-5, aggressive gamble before final ITT
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: needed Valverde to chase Nibali back instead of covering himself, concerning third-week legs
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: matched every acceleration from rivals despite attacking multiple times, exceptional recovery between efforts
